agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Smart Route Optimization is a logistics feature that automates delivery route assignment and dynamic recalculation for drivers, bridging fleet management, delivery fulfillment, and customer communication. It aims to reduce manual dispatch overhead while keeping customers informed and ensuring regulatory/vehicle compliance.","bounded_context":"This feature spans four bounded contexts: (1) Route Optimization — owns route calculation, recalculation triggers, and stop sequencing; (2) Fleet Management — owns driver shifts, vehicle classes, and licensing constraints; (3) Delivery Management — owns delivery lifecycle, zone assignment, and delivery status transitions; (4) Customer Notification — owns notification triggers and customer-facing delivery windows. The feature is an orchestration across these contexts, not a single context itself.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shift","definition":"A bounded work period during which a driver is active and eligible for delivery assignments. Starting a shift is the trigger for initial route assignment."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic region used to partition deliveries and assign them to drivers. A driver operates within one zone per shift."},{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops calculated by the system to minimize travel time or distance within a zone, subject to vehicle class constraints."},{"term":"Route Recalculation","definition":"The process of recomputing the optimized route in response to a defined trigger event (new delivery, failed delivery, driver deviation)."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of vehicle type (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight) that determines which deliveries a driver is eligible to fulfill. This is a hard compliance constraint."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"A rolling proximity window representing the three upcoming delivery stops on a driver's active route, used to trigger customer notifications."},{"term":"Driver Deviation","definition":"When a driver's actual path or stop sequence diverges from the system-suggested optimized route."},{"term":"Estimated Stop Time","definition":"The default time budget allocated per delivery stop (4 minutes), used for ETA calculations. Overridable per zone by the operations team."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery attempt that was unsuccessful (e.g., customer unavailable, access denied), triggering removal from the active route and recalculation."},{"term":"Pending Delivery","definition":"A delivery that has been created and assigned to a zone but not yet completed or failed — eligible for route assignment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically generate an optimized route from all pending deliveries in the driver's assigned zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational automation rule. The business could choose to dispatch manually or change the trigger. It is policy, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver starting a shift with N pending deliveries in their zone, the system produces an ordered route containing exactly those N deliveries within T seconds of shift start."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's active zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy for route freshness. The business could choose batch recalculation intervals instead of real-time.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with an active route, when a new pending delivery is created in their zone, the system produces an updated route that includes the new delivery."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a delivery on the active route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy decision to re-optimize after failure. The business could alternatively just skip the stop without full recalculation.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with an active route containing delivery D, when D is marked as failed, the system produces a new route that excludes D and re-optimizes remaining stops."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The route must be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational policy to adapt to driver behavior. Could be changed to advisory-only without system corruption.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with an active route, when the system detects the driver has deviated from the suggested stop sequence or path, a recalculation is triggered from the driver's current position."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that exceeds or is incompatible with their licensed vehicle class.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a hard compliance and safety constraint. A motorcycle driver physically cannot carry freight, and assigning them one could violate transport regulations and insurance terms. Violating this rule produces a corrupt/illegal state.","testable_assertion":"For every delivery on every driver's route, delivery.requiredVehicleClass must be compatible with driver.licensedVehicleClass. No route — initial or recalculated — may ever contain a violation."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on the driver's active route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Notification policy that could be changed to 2 stops or 5 stops. It is a configurable customer experience decision.","testable_assertion":"When a delivery moves into position 1, 2, or 3 on the driver's active route, and the customer has not already been notified for this route version, a notification is sent to the customer."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated time per delivery stop is 4 minutes, but can be overridden by the operations team on a per-zone basis.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational parameter. The 4-minute default is a tunable business setting, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"ETA calculations use zone.overrideStopTime if set, otherwise default to 4 minutes per stop. The operations team can set and update the override value per zone."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are 5 pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"The system generates an optimized route containing all 5 deliveries and presents it to the driver"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active route with 4 remaining stops","when":"A new delivery is added to their zone","then":"The route is recalculated to include the new delivery, and the driver sees the updated route with 5 stops"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route and delivery at stop 3 is marked as failed","when":"The failure status is recorded","then":"The route is recalculated excluding the failed delivery, and remaining stops are re-optimized"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver is on an active route and completes stop 2 but then drives toward stop 5 instead of stop 3","when":"The system detects the deviation","then":"The route is recalculated from the driver's current position with remaining undelivered stops"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A driver is licensed for motorcycle and a pending delivery requires a freight vehicle","when":"Route generation or recalculation runs","then":"The freight delivery is never included in the motorcycle driver's route"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A driver's active route has deliveries A(stop 1), B(stop 2), C(stop 3), D(stop 4), E(stop 5)","when":"The driver completes stop 1 and the route advances","then":"Customers for B, C, and D receive a 'your delivery is in the next 3 stops' notification"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"The operations team has set the stop time override for Zone-West to 6 minutes","when":"A route is generated for a driver in Zone-West with 5 stops","then":"The total estimated route time uses 6 minutes per stop (30 minutes of stop time) rather than the 4-minute default"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A customer was already notified they are in the 'next 3 stops' window","when":"A route recalculation occurs and they remain in the next 3 stops","then":"The customer is not sent a duplicate notification"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A route recalculation moves a customer OUT of the next 3 stops window","when":"The recalculation completes","then":"No retraction notification is sent (the original notification stands), but the customer's position is updated internally"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are zero pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"The driver is informed that no deliveries are currently available, and no route is generated"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Driver starts shift with zero pending deliveries in their zone","expected_behavior":"No route is generated. Driver is placed in an idle/waiting state. When a delivery arrives in the zone, a route is created at that point."},{"scenario":"All remaining deliveries on a route are marked as failed","expected_behavior":"The route is emptied. Driver returns to idle state or is directed to return to depot, depending on operations policy."},{"scenario":"Route recalculation is triggered while a previous recalculation is still in progress","expected_behavior":"The in-progress calculation should be cancelled or the triggers should be debounced/queued to avoid race conditions and stale routes."},{"scenario":"A new delivery is added to the zone but is incompatible with the active driver's vehicle class","expected_behavior":"The delivery is not added to this driver's route. It remains in the pending pool for assignment to a compatible driver."},{"scenario":"Driver deviates but then returns to the original suggested route","expected_behavior":"A recalculation was already triggered on deviation. The recalculated route is now the active route regardless of whether the driver's new path happens to match the old one."},{"scenario":"Customer notification is triggered but the delivery is immediately failed after entering the 3-stop window","expected_behavior":"The notification was already sent and cannot be retracted. The customer should receive a follow-up notification about the failed delivery."},{"scenario":"Two drivers are active in the same zone simultaneously","expected_behavior":"Deliveries must be partitioned between drivers — a delivery should be on exactly one driver's route, never duplicated across multiple routes."},{"scenario":"Operations team changes zone stop time while drivers have active routes in that zone","expected_behavior":"Active routes should recalculate ETAs using the new stop time. Whether this triggers a full route re-optimization or just an ETA update needs clarification."},{"scenario":"A delivery's zone changes after it was already assigned to a driver's route","expected_behavior":"The delivery should be removed from the current route (triggering recalculation) and become eligible for assignment in its new zone."},{"scenario":"Driver's shift ends while deliveries remain on their route","expected_behavior":"Undelivered stops must be returned to the pending pool for reassignment. They should not be silently dropped."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Optimized routing directly reduces fuel costs, increases deliveries per shift, and reduces overtime. Vehicle class enforcement prevents costly insurance and regulatory violations. The 4-minute stop time default affects capacity planning — an inaccurate default cascades into wrong ETAs and broken customer promises.","operational":"Real-time recalculation increases dispatch efficiency but introduces computational load. Operations team override capability provides necessary flexibility for zones with known constraints (e.g., high-rise buildings requiring longer stop times). Deviation detection requires GPS integration and threshold tuning.","user":"Customers gain transparency through the 3-stop notification window, improving satisfaction and reducing 'where is my delivery' support calls. Drivers benefit from automated route planning but may resist if recalculations feel disruptive or override their local knowledge.","risk":"Vehicle class violation is the highest-risk rule — regulatory fines, insurance voiding, and safety incidents. Frequent route recalculations could cause driver distraction or confusion. Notification timing mismatches (notified but delivery delayed due to recalculation) erode customer trust.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Core workflow change — shift start now triggers automatic routing. Must trust system optimization. Deviation triggers recalculation which may feel intrusive."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive proactive delivery proximity notifications. Higher expectations for delivery accuracy and timing."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"New per-zone configuration responsibility for stop time overrides. Must monitor route optimization quality."},{"group":"Dispatch/Planning","impact":"Reduced manual route planning workload, but must handle edge cases (zone conflicts, vehicle class mismatches) that the system cannot resolve automatically."},{"group":"Compliance/Legal","impact":"Vehicle class enforcement is a regulatory requirement. System must produce audit trails proving no violations occurred."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec provides a solid foundation but leaves several operationally critical details ambiguous: how deviation is detected, what 'optimized' means algorithmically, how multi-driver zone conflicts are resolved, and notification behavior during rapid recalculations. These ambiguities could lead to significantly different implementations depending on interpretation.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class constraint is clear and non-negotiable (BR-5)","The three recalculation triggers are explicitly enumerated","Customer notification trigger at 3-stop window is well-defined","Stop time default and override mechanism is clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["How driver deviation is detected — GPS threshold? Sequence change? Both?","What 'optimized' means — shortest distance, fastest time, priority-weighted, or configurable?","Multi-driver behavior in the same zone — how are deliveries partitioned?","Notification behavior when rapid recalculations cause customers to enter and exit the 3-stop window repeatedly","Whether shift end, break, or other driver state changes affect the route"],"assumptions":["A driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift","Zone assignment is predetermined and not dynamically changed during a shift","Vehicle class compatibility is a binary check, not a ranked preference","The optimization algorithm considers real-time traffic or at minimum road distances, not straight-line distance","Notifications are sent at most once per route version for a given customer"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Driver manually deviates from the suggested route' is undefined — does deviation mean the driver visits stops in a different order, takes a different road between stops, skips a stop, or all of these? The detection mechanism (GPS geofencing, sequence tracking, time-based) fundamentally changes the implementation and the driver experience.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team","Engineering"],"options":["Define deviation as visiting stops out of the suggested sequence only","Define deviation as geographic departure beyond a configurable distance threshold from the planned path","Define deviation as both sequence change and geographic departure"],"recommendation":"Define deviation as stop sequence change (driver visits stop N+2 before stop N+1) as the primary trigger, since this is unambiguous and doesn't require GPS threshold tuning. Geographic deviation can be added later as an enhancement."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Optimized route' is not defined — optimization objective is unspecified. Shortest total distance, fastest estimated time, delivery priority weighting, customer SLA deadlines, or a composite score would all produce different routes. This is the most critical design decision for the feature.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Operations Team","Product"],"options":["Optimize for shortest total travel distance","Optimize for fastest estimated completion time (accounts for traffic)","Optimize for delivery priority or SLA deadline compliance","Allow operations team to configure the optimization objective per zone"],"recommendation":"Default to fastest estimated completion time as the primary objective, with delivery priority as a secondary tiebreaker. This balances operational efficiency with customer commitments. Allow operations team to configure weights per zone as a future enhancement."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-driver zone behavior is unspecified. When two or more drivers start shifts in the same zone, the spec does not clarify how pending deliveries are partitioned. Without this rule, drivers could receive overlapping routes or all deliveries could be assigned to the first driver who starts.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Dispatch/Planning","Customers"],"options":["First driver gets all deliveries; subsequent drivers get only new arrivals","Deliveries are partitioned by geographic sub-clustering within the zone","Deliveries are evenly distributed by count, respecting vehicle class constraints","A dispatcher manually assigns delivery subsets before route optimization runs"],"recommendation":"Partition deliveries by geographic sub-clustering within the zone, respecting vehicle class constraints and balancing load by estimated completion time. This prevents overloading one driver and produces naturally efficient routes."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Smart Route Optimization is a logistics feature that automates delivery route assignment and dynamic recalculation for drivers, bridging fleet management, delivery fulfillment, and customer communication. It aims to reduce manual dispatch overhead while keeping customers informed and ensuring regulatory/vehicle compliance.","bounded_context":"This feature spans four bounded contexts: (1) Route Optimization — owns route calculation, recalculation triggers,

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this logistics platform feature "Smart Route Optimization" carefully before producing my JSON output.

Let me identify the bounded contexts, business rules, and domain language.

Bounded Contexts:
1. Route Optimization - route calculation, recalculation triggers, stop ordering
2. Fleet/Driver Management - driver shifts, vehicle classes, zone assignments
3. Delivery Management - pending deliveries, delivery status, failure handling
4. Customer Notification - notification triggers, "next 3 stops" window
5. Operations Configuration - zone-level overrides, time per stop settings

Business Rules:
1. Auto-assign optimized route on shift start based on pending deliveries in zone


2. Recalculate routes when new deliveries arrive, deliveries fail, or drivers manually deviate
3. Notify customers when their delivery enters the "next 3 stops" window
4. Enforce vehicle class matching between drivers and deliveries for safety and legal compliance
5. Use 4 minutes as the default stop duration, with zone-level customization available

Now I'm identifying the hard constraints versus flexible policies. Vehicle class matching is a critical invariant—it's a safety and legal requirement that can't be violated. Similarly, drivers must have an active shift to receive route assignments. The other elements like the 4-minute default, the 3-stop notification window, and which events trigger recalculation are all configurable policies rather than system invariants.

But there are several ambiguities I need to clarify. What exactly does "optimized" mean—are we minimizing distance, time, delivery priority, or some weighted combination? How do we detect manual deviation—through GPS tracking or driver self-reporting? What qualifies as a failed delivery, and who marks it as such? Where does the vehicle class licensing data come from? Is the "next 3 stops" notification sent once or continuously updated? And what happens if multiple recalculation triggers fire simultaneously, or if recalculation happens while a driver is already executing their route?

Given all these gaps, I'd rate my confidence at around 55% or lower.

Let me analyze this logistics feature description carefully before producing my domain analysis.

The feature is "Smart Route Optimization" for a logistics platform. Let me break down the key elements:

1. Trigger: Driver starts shift → system assigns optimized route
2. Route recalculation triggers: new delivery added, delivery marked failed, driver deviates
3. Customer notification: when delivery enters "next 3 stops" window
4. Vehicle class constraint: drivers can only get deliveries matching their licensed vehicle class
5. Time per stop: 4 minutes default, overridable per zone by ops team

Let me identify bounded contexts:


I'm seeing four main domains here: Route Optimization handling the core routing logic, Fleet/Driver Management covering shifts and vehicle licensing, Delivery Management tracking the lifecycle and zones, and Customer Notification managing when to alert customers. There are some gaps in the spec though—I'm not sure how the system detects when a driver manually deviates, what threshold triggers a recalculation, whether the "next 3 stops" window recalculates with each route change, or how zones are actually defined and whether they can overlap.

The biggest question is what "optimized" actually means in this context—is it minimizing distance, time, or something priority-based? These are genuine ambiguities rather than contradictions, so I'm marking this as an ambiguity-type escalation with confidence at or below 55.

This balances operational efficiency with customer commitments. When two or more drivers start shifts in the same zone, the spec does not clarify how pending deliveries are partitioned. Without this rule, drivers could receive overlapping routes or all deliveries could be assigned to the first driver who starts.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Dispatch/Planning","Customers"],"options":["First driver gets all deliveries; subsequent drivers get only new arrivals","Deliveries are partitioned by geographic sub-clustering within the zone","Deliveries are evenly distributed by count, respecting vehicle class constraints","A dispatcher manually assigns delivery subsets before route optimization runs"],"recommendation":"Partition deliveries by geographic sub-clustering within the zone, respecting vehicle class constraints and balancing load by estimated completion time. This prevents overloading one driver and produces naturally efficient routes."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

trace.result: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Smart Route Optimization is a logistics feature that automates delivery route assignment and dynamic recalculation for drivers, bridging fleet management, delivery fulfillment, and customer communication. It aims to reduce manual dispatch overhead while keeping customers informed and ensuring regulatory/vehicle compliance.","bounded_context":"This feature spans four bounded contexts: (1) Route Optimization — owns route calculation, recalculation triggers, and stop sequencing; (2) Fleet Management — owns driver shifts, vehicle classes, and licensing constraints; (3) Delivery Management — owns delivery lifecycle, zone assignment, and delivery status transitions; (4) Customer Notification — owns notification triggers and customer-facing delivery windows. The feature is an orchestration across these contexts, not a single context itself.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shift","definition":"A bounded work period during which a driver is active and eligible for delivery assignments. Starting a shift is the trigger for initial route assignment."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic region used to partition deliveries and assign them to drivers. A driver operates within one zone per shift."},{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops calculated by the system to minimize travel time or distance within a zone, subject to vehicle class constraints."},{"term":"Route Recalculation","definition":"The process of recomputing the optimized route in response to a defined trigger event (new delivery, failed delivery, driver deviation)."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of vehicle type (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight) that determines which deliveries a driver is eligible to fulfill. This is a hard compliance constraint."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"A rolling proximity window representing the three upcoming delivery stops on a driver's active route, used to trigger customer notifications."},{"term":"Driver Deviation","definition":"When a driver's actual path or stop sequence diverges from the system-suggested optimized route."},{"term":"Estimated Stop Time","definition":"The default time budget allocated per delivery stop (4 minutes), used for ETA calculations. Overridable per zone by the operations team."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery attempt that was unsuccessful (e.g., customer unavailable, access denied), triggering removal from the active route and recalculation."},{"term":"Pending Delivery","definition":"A delivery that has been created and assigned to a zone but not yet completed or failed — eligible for route assignment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically generate an optimized route from all pending deliveries in the driver's assigned zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational automation rule. The business could choose to dispatch manually or change the trigger. It is policy, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver starting a shift with N pending deliveries in their zone, the system produces an ordered route containing exactly those N deliveries within T seconds of shift start."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's active zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy for route freshness. The business could choose batch recalculation intervals instead of real-time.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with an active route, when a new pending delivery is created in their zone, the system produces an updated route that includes the new delivery."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a delivery on the active route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy decision to re-optimize after failure. The business could alternatively just skip the stop without full recalculation.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with an active route containing delivery D, when D is marked as failed, the system produces a new route that excludes D and re-optimizes remaining stops."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The route must be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational policy to adapt to driver behavior. Could be changed to advisory-only without system corruption.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with an active route, when the system detects the driver has deviated from the suggested stop sequence or path, a recalculation is triggered from the driver's current position."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that exceeds or is incompatible with their licensed vehicle class.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a hard compliance and safety constraint. A motorcycle driver physically cannot carry freight, and assigning them one could violate transport regulations and insurance terms. Violating this rule produces a corrupt/illegal state.","testable_assertion":"For every delivery on every driver's route, delivery.requiredVehicleClass must be compatible with driver.licensedVehicleClass. No route — initial or recalculated — may ever contain a violation."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on the driver's active route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Notification policy that could be changed to 2 stops or 5 stops. It is a configurable customer experience decision.","testable_assertion":"When a delivery moves into position 1, 2, or 3 on the driver's active route, and the customer has not already been notified for this route version, a notification is sent to the customer."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated time per delivery stop is 4 minutes, but can be overridden by the operations team on a per-zone basis.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational parameter. The 4-minute default is a tunable business setting, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"ETA calculations use zone.overrideStopTime if set, otherwise default to 4 minutes per stop. The operations team can set and update the override value per zone."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are 5 pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"The system generates an optimized route containing all 5 deliveries and presents it to the driver"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active route with 4 remaining stops","when":"A new delivery is added to their zone","then":"The route is recalculated to include the new delivery, and the driver sees the updated route with 5 stops"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route and delivery at stop 3 is marked as failed","when":"The failure status is recorded","then":"The route is recalculated excluding the failed delivery, and remaining stops are re-optimized"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver is on an active route and completes stop 2 but then drives toward stop 5 instead of stop 3","when":"The system detects the deviation","then":"The route is recalculated from the driver's current position with remaining undelivered stops"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A driver is licensed for motorcycle and a pending delivery requires a freight vehicle","when":"Route generation or recalculation runs","then":"The freight delivery is never included in the motorcycle driver's route"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A driver's active route has deliveries A(stop 1), B(stop 2), C(stop 3), D(stop 4), E(stop 5)","when":"The driver completes stop 1 and the route advances","then":"Customers for B, C, and D receive a 'your delivery is in the next 3 stops' notification"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"The operations team has set the stop time override for Zone-West to 6 minutes","when":"A route is generated for a driver in Zone-West with 5 stops","then":"The total estimated route time uses 6 minutes per stop (30 minutes of stop time) rather than the 4-minute default"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A customer was already notified they are in the 'next 3 stops' window","when":"A route recalculation occurs and they remain in the next 3 stops","then":"The customer is not sent a duplicate notification"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A route recalculation moves a customer OUT of the next 3 stops window","when":"The recalculation completes","then":"No retraction notification is sent (the original notification stands), but the customer's position is updated internally"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are zero pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"The driver is informed that no deliveries are currently available, and no route is generated"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Driver starts shift with zero pending deliveries in their zone","expected_behavior":"No route is generated. Driver is placed in an idle/waiting state. When a delivery arrives in the zone, a route is created at that point."},{"scenario":"All remaining deliveries on a route are marked as failed","expected_behavior":"The route is emptied. Driver returns to idle state or is directed to return to depot, depending on operations policy."},{"scenario":"Route recalculation is triggered while a previous recalculation is still in progress","expected_behavior":"The in-progress calculation should be cancelled or the triggers should be debounced/queued to avoid race conditions and stale routes."},{"scenario":"A new delivery is added to the zone but is incompatible with the active driver's vehicle class","expected_behavior":"The delivery is not added to this driver's route. It remains in the pending pool for assignment to a compatible driver."},{"scenario":"Driver deviates but then returns to the original suggested route","expected_behavior":"A recalculation was already triggered on deviation. The recalculated route is now the active route regardless of whether the driver's new path happens to match the old one."},{"scenario":"Customer notification is triggered but the delivery is immediately failed after entering the 3-stop window","expected_behavior":"The notification was already sent and cannot be retracted. The customer should receive a follow-up notification about the failed delivery."},{"scenario":"Two drivers are active in the same zone simultaneously","expected_behavior":"Deliveries must be partitioned between drivers — a delivery should be on exactly one driver's route, never duplicated across multiple routes."},{"scenario":"Operations team changes zone stop time while drivers have active routes in that zone","expected_behavior":"Active routes should recalculate ETAs using the new stop time. Whether this triggers a full route re-optimization or just an ETA update needs clarification."},{"scenario":"A delivery's zone changes after it was already assigned to a driver's route","expected_behavior":"The delivery should be removed from the current route (triggering recalculation) and become eligible for assignment in its new zone."},{"scenario":"Driver's shift ends while deliveries remain on their route","expected_behavior":"Undelivered stops must be returned to the pending pool for reassignment. They should not be silently dropped."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Optimized routing directly reduces fuel costs, increases deliveries per shift, and reduces overtime. Vehicle class enforcement prevents costly insurance and regulatory violations. The 4-minute stop time default affects capacity planning — an inaccurate default cascades into wrong ETAs and broken customer promises.","operational":"Real-time recalculation increases dispatch efficiency but introduces computational load. Operations team override capability provides necessary flexibility for zones with known constraints (e.g., high-rise buildings requiring longer stop times). Deviation detection requires GPS integration and threshold tuning.","user":"Customers gain transparency through the 3-stop notification window, improving satisfaction and reducing 'where is my delivery' support calls. Drivers benefit from automated route planning but may resist if recalculations feel disruptive or override their local knowledge.","risk":"Vehicle class violation is the highest-risk rule — regulatory fines, insurance voiding, and safety incidents. Frequent route recalculations could cause driver distraction or confusion. Notification timing mismatches (notified but delivery delayed due to recalculation) erode customer trust.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Core workflow change — shift start now triggers automatic routing. Must trust system optimization. Deviation triggers recalculation which may feel intrusive."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive proactive delivery proximity notifications. Higher expectations for delivery accuracy and timing."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"New per-zone configuration responsibility for stop time overrides. Must monitor route optimization quality."},{"group":"Dispatch/Planning","impact":"Reduced manual route planning workload, but must handle edge cases (zone conflicts, vehicle class mismatches) that the system cannot resolve automatically."},{"group":"Compliance/Legal","impact":"Vehicle class enforcement is a regulatory requirement. System must produce audit trails proving no violations occurred."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec provides a solid foundation but leaves several operationally critical details ambiguous: how deviation is detected, what 'optimized' means algorithmically, how multi-driver zone conflicts are resolved, and notification behavior during rapid recalculations. These ambiguities could lead to significantly different implementations depending on interpretation.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class constraint is clear and non-negotiable (BR-5)","The three recalculation triggers are explicitly enumerated","Customer notification trigger at 3-stop window is well-defined","Stop time default and override mechanism is clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["How driver deviation is detected — GPS threshold? Sequence change? Both?","What 'optimized' means — shortest distance, fastest time, priority-weighted, or configurable?","Multi-driver behavior in the same zone — how are deliveries partitioned?","Notification behavior when rapid recalculations cause customers to enter and exit the 3-stop window repeatedly","Whether shift end, break, or other driver state changes affect the route"],"assumptions":["A driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift","Zone assignment is predetermined and not dynamically changed during a shift","Vehicle class compatibility is a binary check, not a ranked preference","The optimization algorithm considers real-time traffic or at minimum road distances, not straight-line distance","Notifications are sent at most once per route version for a given customer"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Driver manually deviates from the suggested route' is undefined — does deviation mean the driver visits stops in a different order, takes a different road between stops, skips a stop, or all of these? The detection mechanism (GPS geofencing, sequence tracking, time-based) fundamentally changes the implementation and the driver experience.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team","Engineering"],"options":["Define deviation as visiting stops out of the suggested sequence only","Define deviation as geographic departure beyond a configurable distance threshold from the planned path","Define deviation as both sequence change and geographic departure"],"recommendation":"Define deviation as stop sequence change (driver visits stop N+2 before stop N+1) as the primary trigger, since this is unambiguous and doesn't require GPS threshold tuning. Geographic deviation can be added later as an enhancement."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Optimized route' is not defined — optimization objective is unspecified. Shortest total distance, fastest estimated time, delivery priority weighting, customer SLA deadlines, or a composite score would all produce different routes. This is the most critical design decision for the feature.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Operations Team","Product"],"options":["Optimize for shortest total travel distance","Optimize for fastest estimated completion time (accounts for traffic)","Optimize for delivery priority or SLA deadline compliance","Allow operations team to configure the optimization objective per zone"],"recommendation":"Default to fastest estimated completion time as the primary objective, with delivery priority as a secondary tiebreaker. This balances operational efficiency with customer commitments. Allow operations team to configure weights per zone as a future enhancement."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-driver zone behavior is unspecified. When two or more drivers start shifts in the same zone, the spec does not clarify how pending deliveries are partitioned. Without this rule, drivers could receive overlapping routes or all deliveries could be assigned to the first driver who starts.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Dispatch/Planning","Customers"],"options":["First driver gets all deliveries; subsequent drivers get only new arrivals","Deliveries are partitioned by geographic sub-clustering within the zone","Deliveries are evenly distributed by count, respecting vehicle class constraints","A dispatcher manually assigns delivery subsets before route optimization runs"],"recommendation":"Partition deliveries by geographic sub-clustering within the zone, respecting vehicle class constraints and balancing load by estimated completion time. This prevents overloading one driver and produces naturally efficient routes."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
